completion_rule: Overall completion requires every canonical requirement to be complete. A requirement is complete only when every declared deliverable exists locally, has been validated against the cited source, has no open blocker, and the faithful run reports zero boxed-LCP fallbacks. Performance and real-time closeout also require a validated physical outcome, controlled affinity, repeated paper-contract sequential results, and a same-machine 1-thread/multicore DART scaling matrix; any claim of beating a paper value must identify the exact source row and use comparable scene, precision, contact, tolerance, outcome, affinity, and fallback contracts.
